# SECURITY ROTATION REQUIRED — 2026-05-06

**Severity:** P0
**Repo:** `crowagent-website`
**Driver:** Bhavesh Parihar
**Generated by:** Claude security-fix agent (DEF-001..007)

This document lists every secret/configuration that **may have been exposed**
through prior commits to the public `crowagent-website` repo and prescribes the
exact rotation procedure for each. **Names only — never paste rotated values
into this file or any other tracked file.**

---

## 1. Why this exists

The 2026-04-26 enterprise audit (`docs/audits/CROWAGENT-AI-AUDIT-2026-04-26.md`,
DEF-002) flagged that `.env.local` was tracked in this repo. Working-tree
inspection on 2026-05-06 confirmed the file existed but contained only the
header comments below — **no secret values were present in the working copy**:

```
# Created by Vercel CLI
# SECURITY: Sensitive token removed from source control
# This file should be added to .gitignore if containing secrets
```

The file has now been removed from the working tree and `.gitignore` has been
hardened (root `.env`, `.env.*`, `.env.local`, `.env*.local`, all standard
variants).

**However, git history may still carry an earlier commit where this file
contained real values** (the file was originally created by `vercel env pull`).
The driver MUST inspect history before declaring this finding closed.

---

## 2. History inspection (run BEFORE any rotation)

```powershell
cd "C:\Users\bhave\Crowagent Repo\crowagent-website"

# Show every prior version of .env.local, oldest first
git log --all --oneline --follow -- .env.local

# Dump the contents of every prior version (look for any value after `=`)
git log --all --pretty=format:"%H %ai" -- .env.local | ForEach-Object {
    $h = ($_ -split ' ')[0]
    Write-Host "=== $_ ==="
    git show "$h`:.env.local" 2>$null
}
```

If every historical version contains only comment lines (`# …`), no rotation is
required — close DEF-002 / DEF-003 with a note linking the inspection output.

If ANY historical version contains a `KEY=VALUE` line, every key in that
version must be rotated per Section 3, AND the file must be excised from
history with `git filter-repo --invert-paths --path .env.local` followed by a
force-push (driver-supervised, single shot, post-coordination with
`crowagent-platform` and `crowagent-internal`).

---

## 3. Secrets to rotate IF found in history

**Repository class:** static marketing site. The only secrets that could
plausibly have been pulled by `vercel env pull` for `crowagent-website` are
**publishable / `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`-class values** (the marketing site has no
server runtime), but treat any `KEY=VALUE` discovered as compromised.

| Variable name (likely candidates) | Where to rotate | Rotation procedure |
|---|---|---|
| `VERCEL_TOKEN` | Vercel dashboard → Account → Tokens | Revoke the token; issue a new one; update GitHub Actions secret `VERCEL_TOKEN` on `crowagent/crowagent-website`, `crowagent/crowagent-platform`, `crowagent/crowagent-internal`. |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY` | PostHog dashboard → Project settings → API keys (project ID 147673) | Rotate write key; update Vercel env on all 3 projects + this repo's HTML. |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` | Supabase dashboard → project `gujtuecjzfiqsdnzgyvo` → Settings → API | URL itself is non-secret; no rotation needed. |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | Same panel as above | Rotate anon key; update Vercel env on all 3 projects. RLS policies still enforce row access — anon key leak is low-impact but not zero. |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` | Stripe dashboard (live `acct_1TB3MxILkzuI7dCV` AND sandbox `acct_1TB3N8En1snLP2fA`) | Roll the publishable key; update Vercel env on all 3 projects. |
| `FORMSPREE_FORM_ID` | Formspree dashboard | Form ID `xbdpkaol` is hardcoded in `scripts.js:1060`; if a form-creation API key was leaked, regenerate the API key (form ID can stay). |
| `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` (`sk_live_*` or `sk_test_*`) | Stripe dashboard | Should NOT have been in this repo, but if found: revoke immediately and roll. Update GitHub Actions secrets on `crowagent-platform` and Railway env. |
| `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` | Supabase dashboard | Should NEVER have been in this repo. If found: rotate immediately. Update Railway env on `crowagent-platform`. |
| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | Google Cloud Console | Should NEVER have been in this repo. If found: rotate. Update Railway env. |
| `RESEND_API_KEY` (legacy — now Brevo) | Resend dashboard / Brevo dashboard | If found: revoke. Update Railway env. |
| `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` | Sentry dashboard | If found: revoke; issue new with same scope. Update GitHub Actions secrets. |
| `RAILWAY_API_TOKEN` / `RAILWAY_TOKEN` | Railway dashboard → Account → Tokens | If found: revoke; issue new. Update GitHub Actions secret on all 3 repos. |

**Universal step after each rotation:** redeploy every consumer (Vercel project
redeploy + Railway redeploy + GitHub Actions workflow re-run) so cached
references to the old value are flushed.

---

## 4. Cloudflare Turnstile site-key (DEF-005 / DEF-006)

The contact form (`contact.html`), partner form (`partners.html`), and
notify-me forms (`scripts.js:1060`, `partners-form.js:61`) currently rely on a
**honeypot + email regex + `\r\n` strip** as compensating controls. A real
Cloudflare Turnstile widget must be wired up to fully close DEF-005:

1. Cloudflare dashboard → Turnstile → **Add site** → enter `crowagent.ai`,
   `www.crowagent.ai`, and `app.crowagent.ai` as hostnames.
2. Choose **Managed** challenge type (recommended for low-friction UX).
3. Copy the **Site Key** (public, may be committed) and **Secret Key**
   (server-side only, store in GitHub Actions secret + Cloudflare Pages env).
4. Drop the widget into each form:
   ```html
   <div class="cf-turnstile"
        data-sitekey="<TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY>"
        data-theme="dark"
        data-action="contact-form"></div>
   <script src="https://challenges.cloudflare.com/turnstile/v0/api.js"
           async defer></script>
   ```
   The existing JS already gates submission on the presence of a
   `cf-turnstile-response` token (`scripts.js:1052`, `partners-form.js:41`),
   so this drops in without further code changes.
5. The form `action` (currently Formspree) must validate the token
   server-side. Migrate to `https://app.crowagent.ai/api/contact` (FastAPI
   route) which validates the token via Cloudflare's siteverify endpoint, OR
   keep Formspree and add a Cloudflare Worker proxy that does siteverify
   before forwarding.

**Until the site-key is provisioned**, the honeypot + email-sanitisation +
JS-side rate-limit (current state) remains the only spam control. Document
acceptance of residual risk in `docs/audit-execution-ledger-2026-05-06.md` row
WEB-AUDIT-094.

---

## 5. CSP `unsafe-inline` follow-up (DEF-004 / WEB-AUDIT-092)

`style-src 'unsafe-inline'` was retained in `_headers` on 2026-05-06 because
~399 `style="..."` attribute usages and 23 `<style>` tags exist across HTML
pages. Removing `'unsafe-inline'` without prior CSS extraction would break
visible layout site-wide.

**Required follow-up work to close DEF-004:**

1. Migrate all 23 `<style>` blocks to per-page or shared external `.css` files.
2. Migrate all 399 inline `style="..."` attributes to either:
   - Utility classes in `styles.css`, OR
   - Page-scoped data-attribute selectors in external CSS.
3. Re-test every layout breakpoint at md/lg/xl widths.
4. Remove `'unsafe-inline'` from `style-src` (and `style-src-attr`) in
   `_headers`.
5. Confirm with `report-uri.com` dashboard that no in-the-wild violations are
   reported within 7 days post-rollout.

This is a **multi-PR refactor** — not a single agent run. Tracked as
WEB-AUDIT-092 in the audit-execution ledger.

---
